title: Tapestry Pub/Sub Messages
description: Schemas for everything passed over a message bus / queue / stream. Two message families: (a) queueable COMMAND messages — the C1 commands (input/action/host-control) framed as bus messages a producer enqueues for an engine session; and (b) SKEIN EVENT messages — the C1 event envelope framed as published events a session emits for subscribers. Where possible these reference the engine command/event protocol schema (C1) rather than duplicating it. ORDERING/DETERMINISM: per session, commands are consumed in a single total order; the same seed plus the same ordered command sequence reproduces a byte-identical event stream, so the command stream is the durable replay input and the event stream is its deterministic projection. Producers MUST therefore order commands within a session by a monotonic sequence and consumers MUST NOT reorder them.

sessionKey.description: The ordering/partition key: the engine session (instance) a message belongs to. All messages for one session share this key so the bus preserves their relative order. Cross-session order is not defined and not required.

topic.description: Logical topic/stream identifier. Convention: `tapestry.commands.<session>` for the inbound command stream of a session, and `tapestry.skein.<session>` for its outbound event stream. A consumer subscribes to a session's skein topic; a producer publishes to its command topic.

seq.description: Monotonic per-session sequence number establishing total order within the session. Required on event messages; recommended on command messages so a broker that cannot guarantee FIFO can re-derive order.

producedAt.description: Producer timestamp (informational; never used for ordering).

traceId.description: Optional correlation id for tracing a request across services.

commandMessage.title: Command message (queueable)
commandMessage.description: A C1 command framed as a bus message and enqueued for a session's engine. The session's FIFO consumer applies commands in one total order. Because a command is pure data, it is durably enqueueable and replayable. Routing/ordering fields (message_type, topic, session, seq, produced_at, trace_id) are common to both message families.

idempotency_key.description: Optional key so a redelivered command is applied at most once.

command.description: The C1 command payload (input / action / query_state / spawn_actor / despawn_actor / move_actor / rotate_actor / snapshot / restore / graph_hash / quit).

eventMessage.title: Skein event message (published)
eventMessage.description: A C1 player-event envelope framed as a published bus message. The ordered sequence of event messages for a session IS the skein (the durable, replayable record). Host-control reply records (e.g. actor_spawned, state, graph_hash) are point-to-point command answers and are NOT part of this published event stream. Routing/ordering fields are common to both message families.

tick.description: The logical tick of the event (mirrors the envelope's tick).

event.description: The C1 event envelope {type:event, tick, event_type, event_name, data}.
